model_evaluation.message: GaussianHMM candidates were scored on a chronological train/test split.

assignment_stability.message: Selected regime count was refit across multiple random seeds and assignments were compared with Adjusted Rand Index.
assignment_stability.interpretation: Higher ARI means separate HMM initializations converge to similar partitions; low ARI means the regime path should be treated as unstable.

model_evaluation.interpretation: Lower BIC rewards fit while penalizing extra states; higher temporal test log-likelihood per observation suggests better out-of-sample sequence fit. This is a diagnostic, not a trading backtest.
model_evaluation.recommendation_reason: 5 regimes has the lowest BIC among scored candidates, so it is the parsimonious default. Use the held-out log-likelihood column as a secondary check rather than as a trading metric.

baseline_suite.name: Rolling-volatility quantile baseline
baseline_suite.description: Primary transparent baseline that buckets observations by rolling volatility only.

baseline_suite.name: EWMA volatility stress baseline
baseline_suite.description: Baseline that uses exponentially weighted volatility, so recent shocks matter more than old observations.

baseline_suite.name: Drawdown stress baseline
baseline_suite.description: Baseline that marks stress when the asset sits in the worst drawdown quintile of the analysis window.

transition_stability.message: First-half and second-half transition matrices were compared for drift.

The strongest held-out log-likelihood candidate was 2, so this is a review point rather than a blind auto-choice.
